IBSI Definition: Ratio of the minor axis to the major axis of the tumor ellipsoid.
Biological Interpretation: Measures elongation. Lower values indicate highly elongated tumors, reflecting asymmetric, aggressive growth along anatomical structures.

IBSI Definition: Maximum voxel value after LLH wavelet filtering.
Biological Interpretation: Reflects localized high-density peaks (e.g. solid components/calcification) in a low-frequency background.

IBSI Definition: Median voxel value after LLH wavelet filtering.
Biological Interpretation: Represents the central density of the tumor after smoothing high-frequency noise horizontally/vertically.

IBSI Definition: Sum of squared voxel values scaled by voxel volume.
Biological Interpretation: Combines tumor volume and density. Large, dense tumors produce high values, indicating overall tumor burden.

IBSI Definition: Asymmetry of voxel intensity distribution about its mean.
Biological Interpretation: Indicates spatial asymmetry in tissue composition (e.g. localized necrotic vs viable tumor regions).

IBSI Definition: Joint emphasis of long runs and high gray-level values.
Biological Interpretation: Represents large, continuous zones of high density (e.g. active solid tumor mass).

IBSI Definition: Variance of local voxel dependency counts.
Biological Interpretation: Indicates variation in local texture density, suggesting micro-environmental structural heterogeneity.
Reflects clear interfaces and high contrast transitions between different tumor tissue types.
High values indicate intensity variations are dominated by extreme values (e.g. microcalcifications/necrotic spots).

IBSI Definition: Maximum voxel value in the horizontal high-frequency band.
Biological Interpretation: Reflects fine-grained high-intensity details or sharp horizontal density transitions.

IBSI Definition: Intensity asymmetry in the HHL wavelet band.
Biological Interpretation: Reflects directional asymmetry in cellular density or vascular patterns.

IBSI Definition: Variability of size zone volumes throughout the tumor.
Biological Interpretation: High values indicate a wide variety of zone sizes, representing highly fragmented, heterogeneous tumor zones.

IBSI Definition: Variability of gray-level intensities across size zones.
Biological Interpretation: Indicates significant variation in density levels across zones, reflecting intratumoral density heterogeneity.

IBSI Definition: Variability of size zone volumes under HHH filtering.
Biological Interpretation: Highly heterogeneous distribution of fine-texture zone sizes, indicating structural fragmentation.

IBSI Definition: Emphasizes small zones of high gray-level values.
Biological Interpretation: Reflects small, highly dense clusters of active cells or microcalcifications, indicating active proliferation.
Indicates complex structural patterns with a mix of very small and very large density zones.

IBSI Definition: Minimum voxel value in the smoothed LLL band.
Biological Interpretation: Represents the lowest density in the smoothed volume, typically corresponding to necrosis or fluid.

IBSI Definition: Difference between max and min voxel values in the LLL band.
Biological Interpretation: Measures the overall range of macro-intensities, reflecting broad density gradients.

IBSI Definition: Emphasizes small dependencies with high gray-level values.
Biological Interpretation: Indicates fine-grained high-density textures in the smoothed volume, reflecting clusters of viable tumor cells.
